events.text: Patient is a 71-year-old Caucasian male, former smoker with 30-pack-year history, quit 5 years ago. ECOG performance status 2. Comorbidities include atrial fibrillation on apixaban, coronary artery disease status post stent placement, and chronic kidney disease stage 3.

Diagnosed with Stage IV non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), adenocarcinoma histology (ICD-O code 8140/3), primary site right upper lobe (C34.1). TNM staging T3N3M1b with contralateral mediastinal lymphadenopathy, bilateral adrenal metastases, and hepatic metastases.

Endobronchial ultrasound-guided transbronchial needle aspiration (EBUS-TBNA) of station 7 lymph node reveals metastatic adenocarcinoma. TTF-1 positive, Napsin A positive, CK7 positive. Cell block adequate for molecular testing. Poorly differentiated with solid and micropapillary patterns.

NGS (FoundationOne CDx) on lymph node biopsy specimen. EGFR L858R mutation detected (VAF 40%). TP53 R175H hotspot mutation (VAF 42%). STK11 frameshift mutation (VAF 22%). No ALK, ROS1, BRAF, MET, RET, or KRAS alterations. PD-L1 TPS 1%. TMB 4 mutations/Mb.

Assessment: Stage IV EGFR L858R-mutant adenocarcinoma with high disease burden including hepatic metastases. Poor performance status. Multiple comorbidities. Plan: Start erlotinib 150 mg daily (renal dosing consideration given CKD). Monitor renal function closely. Supportive care.
Started erlotinib 150 mg orally once daily for EGFR L858R-mutant Stage IV NSCLC.

Baseline CT shows 4.8 cm right upper lobe mass, bulky mediastinal and bilateral hilar lymphadenopathy, bilateral adrenal masses (2.5 cm right, 1.8 cm left), and multiple hepatic metastases (largest 3.2 cm right hepatic lobe).
MRI brain shows single 12 mm enhancing lesion in the left parietal lobe, consistent with brain metastasis.
Stereotactic radiosurgery to left parietal brain metastasis, 20 Gy in single fraction. Short course of dexamethasone 4 mg BID with taper.
Grade 2 rash on face and trunk. Grade 1 diarrhea. Grade 1 elevated creatinine (from baseline 1.4 to 1.7 mg/dL). Monitored renal function closely.

Restaging CT at 6 weeks shows right upper lobe mass decreased from 4.8 cm to 3.6 cm. Hepatic lesions decreased (largest now 2.4 cm). Adrenal lesions slightly decreased. Partial response despite high disease burden.

Assessment: Partial response to erlotinib at 6 weeks. Brain metastasis treated with SRS. Renal function stable. ECOG improved to 1. Plan: Continue erlotinib. Monitor renal function. Repeat brain MRI in 3 months.
MRI brain at 3 months shows treated left parietal lesion decreased with central necrosis consistent with treatment effect. No new brain lesions.
CT at 4 months shows continued partial response. Right upper lobe mass 3.0 cm. Hepatic metastases stable to decreased.
CT at 7 months shows stable disease. Right upper lobe mass 3.1 cm. Hepatic lesions stable. Adrenal lesions stable.
CT at 10 months shows interval increase in hepatic metastases with new lesions. Right upper lobe mass increased to 4.0 cm. New small bilateral pleural effusions. Progressive disease.

Assessment: Disease progression on erlotinib at 10 months. Plan: Liquid biopsy to check for T790M. Given comorbidities and ECOG 2, discuss goals of care and whether patient is candidate for further systemic therapy.
Liquid biopsy (Guardant360) detects EGFR L858R (VAF 18.5%) and EGFR T790M (VAF 4.2%). TP53 R175H retained. T790M resistance confirmed.
Discontinued erlotinib. Started osimertinib 80 mg daily for T790M-positive progressive disease.
Grade 1 QTc prolongation on osimertinib (QTc 475 ms from baseline 440 ms). Cardiology consulted given history of atrial fibrillation. Continued with ECG monitoring.
Restaging CT at 6 weeks on osimertinib shows decrease in hepatic metastases and right upper lobe mass decreased to 3.2 cm. Pleural effusions stable. Partial response.

Assessment: Response to osimertinib in second line. QTc being monitored. Overall functional status improved. Plan: Continue osimertinib with cardiac monitoring every 3 months. Repeat imaging in 3 months.

scenario_blurb: Stage IIIB-IV non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) with EGFR activating mutations (L858R or exon 19 deletion). Patients treated with EGFR TKIs (erlotinib, osimertinib) with eventual T790M resistance. Include NGS testing, brain metastases in some patients.
